children.name: Giorgia di Lacon-Gunale, Marchioness of Massa

km.Italy: ប្រទេសអ៊ីតាលី

zh.lifeSummary: Sardinia de Zori di Arborea於1100年之前與Constantine II Salusio III de Lacon-Gunale, 5th Judge of Cagliari在義大利結婚。他們至少是3個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sardinia de Zori di Arborea于1100年之前与Constantine II Salusio III de Lacon-Gunale, 5th Judge of Cagliari在意大利结婚。他们至少是3个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Zori
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: diminutive or patronymic from the old personal name or nickname Zor, based on zor ‘aurora, dawn’, also ‘daybreak’, or perhaps from a short form of the related personal name Zorislav (see Zoric 1).
(Zorč): patronymic of the same origin as 1 above, or perhaps a derivative of the Friulian personal name Zorz (see George ). Compare Zorzi 2.

variants.name: Zoric
meanings: Croatian and Serbian (Zorić), Slovenian (Zorič): metronymic from the female personal name Zora, from zora ‘aurora, dawn’, or a patronymic from a short form of the South Slavic compound name Zorislav, based on the same word.
Slovenian (Zorič): patronymic from the old personal name or nickname Zor ‘born at dawn’.

nameMeanings.name: di
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mandarin form of the surname 狄, a term referring to the tribes in ancient northern China: (i) from Di (狄), the name of an ancient state (thought to have been located in Hebei or Shandong province), which was granted to Xiao Bo or Kao Bo by King Cheng of Zhou (reigned 1042–1021  BC ). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Ku Di (庫狄) family during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) traced back to other minority ethnic groups in ancient northwestern China.

Mandarin form of the Chinese surname 邸, meaning ‘official residence’ in Chinese: (i) from placename Di (邸), the name of a county (unidentified location). (ii) said to be borne by some descendants of Kujula Kadphises (called 丘就郤 or 邸就郤 in Chinese, 30–c. 80 AD ), founder of the Kushan Empire (one branch of the Yuezhi people originating from western China) located in Central Asia during the Eastern Han dynasty (25–220 AD ).

variants.name: Chi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Dong, Yun, Cheng, Jae, Yong, Cheng Chung, Chi, Chun, Duk, Fang, Feng.  Korean Chung, Chong, Jae Hong, Moon, Myong, Pong, Shen, Yoo, Byung, Chang, Chol, Chong Hun.  Southeast Asian Phi, Hung, Ly, Tu, Denh, Do Van, Duong, Hao, Kieu, Mui, Nga, Phat.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 池, meaning ‘moat’ in ancient Chinese: (i) acquired as a surname by people who lived beside a moat. (ii) from Chi (池), said to be the name of an ancient state (of unknown location and period). (iii) from the personal name Chi (池), personal name of a son of an official in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 遲, meaning ‘late’ in Chinese: (i) borne by descendants of Chi Ren (遲任), a person who lived during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the Chinese compound surname Yu-Chi (尉遲), originally the name of a tribe from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iii) from the second element of the personal name Zi Chi (子遲), style name of Fan Xu, a student of Confucius (551–479  BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Sardinia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Mario, Aurelio, Carlos, Casimiro, Domingos, Ferando, Jaime, Juan, Luiz, Palmira.  Portuguese Joao.
Portuguese: from sardinha ‘sardine’, a metonymic occupational name for a sardine seller, or possibly a nickname.

en.lifeSummary: Sardinia de Zori di Arborea married Constantine II Salusio III de Lacon-Gunale, 5th Judge of Cagliari before 1100, in Italy. They were the parents of at least 3 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Sardinia de Zori di Arborea a épousé Constantine II Salusio III de Lacon-Gunale, 5th Judge of Cagliari avant 1100, à Italie. Ils ont eu au moins 3 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Sardinia de Zori di Arborea hat vor 1100 in Italien Constantine II Salusio III de Lacon-Gunale, 5th Judge of Cagliari geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Sardinia de Zori di Arborea lei sposato Constantine II Salusio III de Lacon-Gunale, 5th Judge of Cagliari prima del 1100, nel Italia. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figlie.

ja.lifeSummary: Sardinia de Zori di Arboreaは1100年以前にイタリアでConstantine II Salusio III de Lacon-Gunale, 5th Judge of Cagliariと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Sardinia de Zori di Arborea(은)는 1100년 이전 이탈리아에서 Constantine II Salusio III de Lacon-Gunale, 5th Judge of Cagliari(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sardinia de Zori di Arborea casou-se com Constantine II Salusio III de Lacon-Gunale, 5th Judge of Cagliari antes 1100, em Itália. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhas.

ru.lifeSummary: Sardinia de Zori di Arborea вышла замуж за человека по имени Constantine II Salusio III de Lacon-Gunale, 5th Judge of Cagliari перед 1100 г. в местности Италия. У них было по крайней мере 3 дочери.

es.lifeSummary: Sardinia de Zori di Arborea se casó con Constantine II Salusio III de Lacon-Gunale, 5th Judge of Cagliari antes de 1100, en Italia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijas.
